skill.name: release-orchestrator
skill.description: Coordinate software release preparation, rollout readiness, migration notes, rollback planning, stakeholder communication, and final go/no-go release packets. Use when asked to prepare a release packet, review release readiness, or turn scattered launch notes into one reusable release workflow. Do not use for generic project planning, one-off announcement drafting, or high-level release theory.
skill.summary: Turn scattered release notes into a single, reusable release packet with rollout, rollback, migration, and stakeholder coverage.
skill.author: yaojingang

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 28 static findings across 12 files (386 lines). All 5 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are false positives triggered by words like 'hardening' in description fields. All 23 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are false positives caused by Markdown backtick syntax for code spans and fenced code blocks. The Python script only reads a JSON file and prints formatted Markdown output. No prompt injection, network calls, eval, or dangerous code patterns were found.

low_findings.title: Markdown backticks misinterpreted as shell execution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 23 backtick occurrences as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are all standard Markdown syntax: inline code spans (e.g., `references/release-checklist.md`) and fenced code blocks (e.g., ```md at references/migration-template.md:15). No actual code execution occurs.

low_findings.title: False positive: 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' keyword match
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 5 files for 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' based on keyword heuristics (e.g., the word 'hardening' in descriptions, hex-like strings in JSON). No actual cryptographic code or algorithms exist in this skill.

content.value_statement: Release coordination is fragmented across checklists, chat threads, and scattered documents. This skill consolidates release inputs into one reusable packet with rollout, rollback, migration, and stakeholder coverage.
actual_capabilities: Generates structured release packets with scope, risks, migrations, rollout, and rollback sections
Applies severity-based risk classification (low, medium, high, critical) using a built-in risk matrix
Produces explicit go/no-go decisions with justified conditions
Formats stakeholder communication blocks tailored by audience and timing
Assembles deterministic release packets from JSON input via an included Python script
limitations: Does not execute or automate actual deployments; it only produces documentation
Does not integrate with CI/CD pipelines, ticketing systems, or monitoring tools
Cannot fetch live release data; relies entirely on user-provided inputs
Output quality depends on completeness of the release inputs supplied

use_cases.title: Prepare a release packet before launch
use_cases.description: Engineering leads compile scope, risks, migrations, rollout steps, and rollback triggers into a single reusable packet for stakeholder review.

use_cases.title: Review release readiness for go/no-go
use_cases.description: Staff engineers verify that migration notes, rollback criteria, and stakeholder messages are present and consistent before approving a production push.

use_cases.title: Standardize stakeholder communication
use_cases.description: Product managers generate audience-specific announcements (on-call, support, leadership) with appropriate timing and messaging for each release.

prompt_templates.title: Basic release packet from notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use release-orchestrator to turn these release notes into a full release packet: [paste your notes here]. Include scope, risks, migrations, rollout, rollback, and a final decision.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: Quick packet from unstructured notes

prompt_templates.title: Structured packet from JSON input
prompt_templates.prompt: Run scripts/build_release_packet.py with input/release_input_example.json as the shape reference. Fill in my actual release details and produce the formatted Markdown packet.

prompt_templates.title: Readiness review with risk matrix
prompt_templates.prompt: Read references/release-checklist.md and references/risk-matrix.md. Review my release inputs against the required sections and flag any blockers before I propose a go decision.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-audience stakeholder plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Read references/stakeholder-comms.md. Draft tailored messages for on-call engineering, customer support, and leadership with timing and escalation paths for this release.

output_examples.input: Release name: 2026.03.31-search-hardening. Scope: query guardrails and index migration. One high risk: migration must complete before traffic shift.
output_examples.output: A structured release packet with sections for Scope, Risks (severity-tagged), Migrations, Rollout steps, Rollback triggers, Stakeholder messages per audience, and a final decision of GO WITH CONDITIONS with justification.

output_examples.input: I have migration notes and rollout steps but no rollback triggers defined.
output_examples.output: The skill flags the missing rollback triggers as a blocker and recommends NO-GO until concrete triggers are supplied, rather than fabricating readiness.

best_practices: Provide complete release inputs (scope, risks, migrations, rollout, rollback, audiences) before requesting a packet
Reference the risk matrix and checklist files to ensure all required sections are covered
Use the deterministic script when you need reproducible, reviewable packet assembly
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for generic project planning or brainstorming unrelated to release packaging
Do not request a GO decision when migration or rollback details are missing; the skill will flag blockers
Do not use it for single announcement drafting; it is designed for full release packet assembly

faq.question: Does this skill automate deployments?
faq.answer: No. It produces documentation and structured release packets only. Actual deployment automation requires your existing CI/CD tooling.

faq.question: What inputs does it need?
faq.answer: Release scope, risky changes, migrations, rollout sequencing, rollback triggers, and communication audiences. Missing inputs are flagged as blockers.

faq.question: Can it integrate with Jira or PagerDuty?
faq.answer: No. It does not connect to external systems. It generates Markdown content that you can paste into your existing tools.

faq.question: How does it decide GO vs NO-GO?
faq.answer: It applies the built-in risk matrix: NO-GO if migration or rollback details are missing; GO WITH CONDITIONS if risks remain with explicit mitigations; GO when no high/critical risks remain.

faq.question: Is the Python script safe to run?
faq.answer: Yes. It reads a JSON file you provide and prints formatted Markdown to stdout. It makes no network calls and does not execute arbitrary code.

faq.question: Which AI tools does it support?
faq.answer: It is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as listed in the supported tools field.
